message:
webgpu: cooperative X tile in MATMUL_INT4_FP16_F16

Adds a workgroup-shared x_tile (TR × BLOCK = 128 floats) so the four
T rows × 32 K-block elements are loaded into on-chip memory once per
workgroup, instead of issuing 64 × 4 = 256 redundant global X loads
per K block.

Cumulative GPU optimization (original → now), median of 10 forwards
on M-series Chromium:

  T=16:  22.1 →  9.1 ms  (2.4x)
  T=32:  39.8 → 13.5 ms  (2.9x)
  T=64:  77.2 → 22.7 ms  (3.4x)
  T=128: 154.6 → 46.9 ms (3.3x)
  T=256: 309.7 → 95.3 ms (3.2x)

The naive 1-thread-per-output structure has now hit the practical
ceiling without a more invasive rewrite (subgroup-cooperative K
reduction, operator fusion across kernels, or matrix-accelerator
intrinsics like Apple AMX which WebGPU doesn't expose). Each tweak
below this point lands in the ~5-10% per-change band — well inside
bench measurement noise for end-to-end forwards.

Parity unchanged: argmax 32/32, rms=0.18.
